intro.title: A portfolio that feels
intro.lede: I am Peter Mwaura, an engineering student who builds across code, hardware, and storytelling. I like work that blends technical rigor with clear communication and memorable product thinking.

overviewItems.label: Best Fit
overviewItems.value: Backend, product engineering, and software internships

overviewItems.label: Approach
overviewItems.value: Prototype quickly, explain clearly, iterate with intent

intro.recruiterHeading: I build with range, but I present with focus.
intro.recruiterBody: This site is structured like a company website on purpose. Each page isolates a clear story: who I am, what I care about, what I have built, and how I think.

about.title: Built by curiosity, sharpened by iteration.
about.lede: I enjoy being in the middle of messy, real-world problems. I like finding structure, testing quickly, and shaping technical work into something people can trust and understand.
about.workingStyleTitle: I like being the person who turns ambiguity into momentum.
workingStyleParagraphs: My strongest work happens when a team needs both technical depth and clarity. I enjoy translating between disciplines and helping a project stay legible as it grows.
I care about taste in engineering: not just whether something works, but whether it is easy to maintain, easy to understand, and clearly aligned with the problem it solves.
recruiterNotes: Comfortable across software and systems thinking
Able to explain technical tradeoffs clearly

education.title: B.S. Candidate in Engineering
education.description: Coursework spanning programming, systems design, and human-centered engineering.

education.title: Product Engineering Intern
education.description: Built internal tools, supported experiments, and helped shape technical communication.

education.title: Design Lead in Student Projects
education.description: Coordinated prototype reviews and pushed ideas from concept to testable systems.

about.quote: Great engineering is not only functional. It is legible, adaptable, and persuasive.
about.quoteBody: That is the lens I bring to every project, whether I am writing backend code, building tools, or pitching the outcome.

interests.title: I am drawn to systems that feel alive.
interests.lede: The projects I choose are usually shaped by the same themes: automation, interfaces, resilience, scalable infrastructure, and tools that help people make better decisions.

cards.title: Infrastructure that stays calm under pressure.
cards.description: I enjoy the architecture behind products: APIs, data flow, reliability, and clean service boundaries.

cards.title: Clear control surfaces for complex systems.
cards.description: Dashboards and admin tools fascinate me because they turn complexity into something intuitive and usable.

cards.title: Engineering that reduces repetitive friction.
cards.description: I like building systems that take manual overhead out of a workflow without making the result feel opaque.

favorites: Turning raw data into operational insight
Building first-pass prototypes with clear feedback loops
Connecting services to thoughtful admin interfaces
Designing systems that explain themselves well
outsideParagraphs: I like dissecting product launches and studying how strong technical teams communicate the value of what they build.
That is a big reason this portfolio leans into a studio aesthetic: it reflects the type of engineering culture I want to grow in.

projects.title: Execution across backend, software, and story.
projects.lede: These projects are written the way recruiters and hiring managers usually need them: fast to scan, clear on ownership, and specific about what was actually built.

items.title: Portfolio CMS and Media Studio
items.description: Built a small editable content system that lets a personal site update from a browser-based admin without manually touching the page files.
bullets: Created a lightweight content API and JSON-backed storage layer
Built an admin editor for text, media, and page backgrounds
Connected multi-page public views to shared structured content
items.role: Backend design, content modeling, admin tooling

items.title: Event and Operations API
items.description: Designed a backend service for managing schedules, registrations, and internal dashboards for a student organization with growing operational needs.
bullets: Modeled the data structure for members, events, and registrations
Designed API endpoints for admin workflows and reporting
Focused on maintainability, role separation, and admin usability

items.title: Operations Insight Dashboard
items.description: Created a lightweight dashboard experience to turn raw operational data into decisions, with a focus on clarity for non-technical stakeholders.
bullets: Cleaned and structured data for trend visibility
Framed outputs for quick recruiter and sponsor comprehension
Balanced interface clarity with engineering detail

more.title: Additional context around how I show up.
more.lede: A few of the organizations, recognitions, and mentoring roles that shape how I contribute beyond a single project.

affiliations.label: Campus
affiliations.title: Engineering and builder communities
affiliations.description: Active in student spaces where technical projects, peer learning, and collaborative builds are part of the culture.

affiliations.label: Professional
affiliations.title: Cross-disciplinary engineering communities
affiliations.description: I gravitate toward groups that blend technical depth, communication, and collaborative problem-solving.

awards.label: Recognition
awards.title: Project and presentation distinction
awards.description: Recognition for work that paired technical execution with clear storytelling and strong delivery.

awards.label: Academic
awards.title: Growth through ambitious coursework
awards.description: Academic milestones that reflect persistence, curiosity, and a willingness to take on demanding technical work.

mentorship.label: Peer Support
mentorship.title: Helping others ramp faster
mentorship.description: I enjoy making technical work more approachable through walkthroughs, documentation, and practical feedback.

mentorship.label: Community
mentorship.title: Creating momentum in team environments
mentorship.description: Mentorship, to me, is often about reducing confusion and helping collaborators move with more confidence.

contact.title: Open to teams that value range, speed, and clarity.
contact.lede: I am currently looking for engineering internship opportunities in backend, software, product engineering, or multidisciplinary build teams.
contact.reachOutTitle: Let's talk about what your team is building.
contact.reachOutBody: If you are hiring for roles where curiosity and execution both matter, I would love to connect.
